title: Gen Z investors are regularly turning to AI for advice — and that could soon be a huge problem
excerpt: Gen Z turns to AI for financial advice, raising trust concerns
content_html:
• Report finds 80% of Gen Z investors now rely on AI for guidance, 58% would let it advise more actively
  • AI tools are democratizing investing because they’re free/cheap and convenient
  • Transparency, citations, and clear safeguards are still in demand

New data from Opinium has revealed 80% Gen Z investors now use AI for investment-related guidance, putting them far ahead of Baby Boomers (17%) and Gen X (47%).

But younger investors seem to be using it much more than just educational reasons, such as explaining terminology or researching investments – they’re even using it in actual decision-making.

The report added nearly three in five (58%) of Gen Z investors would be likely to use AI to decide where to invest their money. While this is still lower than the total number of younger investors who would use AI, it still means more than half are trusting tools like ChatGPT and Gemini with how they allocate their money.

Is AI democratizing investing for everybody?

The report also quantifies the generational gap in trust, with only 29% of Gen Z investors not trusting AI-generated information compared with 49% across all age brackets. But trust actually rises after users have experienced artificial intelligence, with 72% of users noting they trust it.

What the data also implies is that artificial intelligence has made investing more accessible to more people – 43% say they started using AI tools because it was free, and 41% valued its speed and convenience.

More than a third (38%) of female investors said they felt more comfortable asking AI than a person compared with 27% of male investors, highlighting the technology’s impacts in democratizing investing.

Opinium notes that AI chatbots could be helping to reduce the embarrassment associated with beginners asking basic questions.

Sign up to the TechRadar Pro newsletter to get all the top news, opinion, features and guidance your business needs to succeed!

Trust is more important than ever where finances are invovled

But when it comes to trusting machines with financial decisions, users still demand high levels of trust. The most frequently cited trust-building measures include seeking evidence that the information is both accurate and current (28%), cross-checking it against data elsewhere (25%) and transparency about the sources used (23%).

“Trust still depends on transparency,” Opinium Associate Director James Nicandrou wrote. “Investors want to know that information is accurate and easy to verify.”

“As AI becomes more widely used in financial decision-making, clear safeguards and signposting will be crucial to helping people use these tools confidently and responsibly.”

All in all, the study shows how younger people are increasingly beginning their investment journeys by asking AI assistants to explain concepts and compare options, but the same trust and accountability that enterprises are battling when trying to implement organization-wide AI is also proving to be just as important for the end user.

While the precise figures differ, Yonder research with the FCA also found that consumers are increasingly using AI for personal finances – 72% said they use it to summarize and explain information, while 61% ask it to suggest actions on their behalves.

Earlier Freetrade research also sets the foundations, noting how 91% of its respondents lacked confidence in investing. One of its suggestions was to improve education for younger adults, with senior analyst Dan Lane noting that “the greatest advantage you can give your investments is time.” With the latest trends revealing that younger adults are getting to grips with investing thanks to AI, accessibility certainly looks to be improving.

content_html_zh:
• 报告发现 80% 的 Z 世代投资者现在依赖人工智能进行指导，58% 的人愿意让它更积极地提供建议
  • 人工智能工具正在使投资民主化，因为它们免费/便宜且方便
  • 透明度、引用和明确的保障措施仍然存在需求

Opinium 的新数据显示，80% 的 Z 世代投资者现在使用人工智能进行投资相关指导，远远领先于婴儿潮一代 (17%) 和 X 世代 (47%)。

但年轻投资者似乎不仅仅出于教育原因使用它，例如解释术语或研究投资，他们甚至在实际决策中使用它。

该报告补充说，近五分之三 (58%) 的 Z 世代投资者可能会使用人工智能来决定将资金投资到哪里。 虽然这仍然低于使用人工智能的年轻投资者总数，但这仍然意味着超过一半的人信任 ChatGPT 和 Gemini 等工具来分配资金。

人工智能正在使每个人的投资民主化吗？

该报告还量化了信任方面的代际差距，只有 29% 的 Z 世代投资者不相信人工智能生成的信息，而所有年龄段的这一比例为 49%。 但在用户体验了人工智能之后，信任度实际上有所上升，72% 的用户表示他们信任它。

数据还表明，人工智能让更多人更容易进行投资——43% 的人表示他们开始使用人工智能工具是因为它是免费的，41% 的人看重其速度和便利性。

超过三分之一 (38%) 的女性投资者表示，与 27% 的男性投资者相比，她们更愿意向人工智能提问，这突显了该技术对民主化投资的影响。

Opinium 指出，人工智能聊天机器人可以帮助减少初学者提出基本问题所带来的尴尬。

订阅 TechRadar Pro 时事通讯，获取您的企业成功所需的所有热门新闻、观点、功能和指导！

在涉及财务的地方，信任比以往任何时候都更加重要

但当涉及到信任机器进行财务决策时，用户仍然需要高度的信任。 最常被提及的建立信任措施包括寻求证据证明信息准确且最新（28%）、与其他地方的数据进行交叉核对（25%）以及所用来源的透明度（23%）。

“信任仍然取决于透明度，”Opinium 副总监 James Nicandrou 写道。 “投资者希望知道信息准确且易于验证。”

“随着人工智能在金融决策中的应用越来越广泛，明确的保障措施和路标对于帮助人们自信、负责任地使用这些工具至关重要。”

总而言之，这项研究表明，越来越多的年轻人通过要求人工智能助手解释概念和比较选项来开始他们的投资之旅，但事实证明，企业在尝试实施全组织人工智能时所面临的信任和责任对于最终用户同样重要。

虽然具体数字有所不同，但 Yonder 与 FCA 的研究还发现，消费者越来越多地使用人工智能来处理个人财务——72% 的人表示他们用人工智能来总结和解释信息，而 61% 的人要求人工智能代表他们提出行动建议。

早期的自由贸易研究也奠定了基础，指出 91% 的受访者对投资缺乏信心。 其中一项建议是改善年轻人的教育，高级分析师丹·莱恩指出，“投资所能带来的最大优势就是时间。” 最新趋势表明，年轻人正在通过人工智能来掌握投资，可访问性肯定会有所改善。

在 Google 新闻上关注 TechRadar，并将我们添加为首选来源，以便在您的 Feed 中获取我们的专家新闻、评论和意见。
